description.task: Create an Animal base class and two subclasses (Dog and Cat) using the extends keyword. Each subclass must override a makeSound() method.
instructions: Define class Dog extends Animal with a constructor that calls super(name).
Override makeSound() in Dog to print '<name> says: Woof!'.
Define class Cat extends Animal, same pattern but prints '<name> says: Meow!'.
description.hint: Use the @Override annotation above overridden methods as a best practice. Call super(name) in subclass constructors to initialise the parent's field.
description.expectedOutput:
Generic Animal makes a sound.
Buddy says: Woof!
Whiskers says: Meow!

description.task: Create a Vehicle class with a describe() method, then extend it with ElectricVehicle that calls super.describe() and adds battery range info.
description.context: The super keyword lets a subclass call the parent class's constructor or methods, avoiding code duplication.
instructions: In ElectricVehicle's constructor, call super(brand, year) to initialise the parent fields, then assign rangeKm.
Override describe(): call super.describe() first, then print 'Range: 500 km'.
description.hint: super() must be the first statement in a subclass constructor. super.describe() calls the parent's version of the method.

description.task: Build a three-level inheritance chain: Person → Employee → Manager. Each level adds a field and overrides toString().
description.context: Inheritance enables a hierarchy where common attributes live in parent classes and specialised attributes are added by subclasses.
instructions: Implement Employee extending Person as described in the comments.
Implement Manager extending Employee as described.
Override toString() at each level with the exact formats shown.
description.hint: System.out.println(object) calls the object's toString() automatically.
description.expectedOutput:
Person: Alice
Employee: Bob | Dept: Engineering
Manager: Carol | Dept: Engineering | Team: 8
